soilType: Well-draining potting mix with peat moss
description: A popular tropical climbing plant native to Central America, known for its large, fenestrated leaves. Grows vigorously in the right conditions and can reach impressive sizes.
careSummary: An easy-going plant that thrives in bright indirect light with regular watering. Its iconic split leaves develop as the plant matures.

description: A hardy succulent native to West Africa with tall, upright sword-like leaves featuring distinctive yellow margins and green banding.
careSummary: Nearly indestructible plant that tolerates low light and infrequent watering. An excellent choice for beginners or low-light spaces.

soilType: Rich, well-draining potting mix with perlite
description: A striking tree from western Africa with large, violin-shaped leaves. Known as the statement plant of the houseplant world, it grows well in bright indoor spaces.
careSummary: Needs bright indirect light and consistent watering. Known for being dramatic when conditions change but rewarding with stunning foliage.

description: A popular trailing houseplant native to South Africa with arching grassy leaves. Produces small white flowers and plantlets (spiderettes) on long stems.
careSummary: Very forgiving plant that produces offshoots abundantly. Great in hanging baskets and safe for pets.

soilType: Cactus and succulent mix, very well-draining
description: A succulent species native to the Arabian Peninsula with thick, fleshy leaves containing a gel used for medicinal and cosmetic purposes.
careSummary: Loves bright sun and minimal water. The gel inside its leaves has soothing properties for minor burns and skin irritations.

description: A tropical plant from Bolivia with large, round, pale green leaves decorated with silver stripes. Known for nyctinasty — leaves fold up at night.
careSummary: Requires high humidity and consistent moisture. A diva among houseplants but its stunning foliage makes the effort worthwhile.

description: A versatile climbing vine native to the Solomon Islands with heart-shaped green leaves marbled in yellow. Thrives in a wide range of conditions.
careSummary: One of the easiest houseplants — tolerates low light, neglect, and irregular watering. Perfect for offices and low-light corners.

description: A tropical perennial native to Eastern Africa with glossy dark green compound leaves arising from thick rhizomes. Extremely drought-tolerant.
careSummary: Thrives on neglect — can go weeks without water and tolerates very low light. A bulletproof choice for any space.

soilType: Rich, moisture-retentive mix with peat moss
description: A lush fern species native to tropical regions with gracefully arching fronds covered in small leaflets. Classic hanging basket plant.
careSummary: Loves humidity and consistent moisture. Benefits from daily misting and does not tolerate dry air or dry soil.

description: A popular flowering houseplant from the tropical Americas with dark green glossy leaves and elegant white spathe flowers.
careSummary: Tolerates low light and tells you when it needs water by drooping dramatically. Produces beautiful white flowers regularly.

description: An iconic tree from Madagascar with thin, arching leaves edged in red atop slender, branching trunks. Striking architectural form.
careSummary: Very adaptable to different light conditions. Prefers to dry out between waterings and is quite forgiving.

description: A compact plant native to South America with thick, succulent-like glossy leaves. Perfect for small spaces and desks.
careSummary: Compact and easy-care with thick leaves that store water. Tolerates some neglect and stays small, making it ideal for tabletops.

description: A spectacular flowering plant from South Africa with large paddle-shaped leaves and iconic orange and blue bird-like flowers.
careSummary: Needs bright light to bloom and plenty of space to grow. Mature plants reward with exotic flowers that resemble tropical birds.

description: A low-growing Brazilian plant with oval leaves featuring striking red veins and dark green patterns. Leaves fold up at night in a prayer-like position.
careSummary: Beautiful patterned leaves that move throughout the day. Needs consistent moisture and humidity but is safe for pets.

soilType: Very gritty cactus mix with pumice
description: A classic desert cactus with flat, oval pads that grow in branching segments. Native to the Americas and remarkably drought-tolerant.
careSummary: Thrives on full sun and very little water. A true desert plant that rewards neglect with robust growth.

description: A tropical plant from Colombia with dark heart-shaped leaves and showy red spathe flowers with a yellow spadix. Blooms year-round in good conditions.
careSummary: Requires high humidity, bright indirect light, and consistently moist soil. Blooms continuously when happy.

description: A charming plant from southwestern China with distinctive round, coin-shaped leaves on long petioles. Known for producing abundant offsets.
careSummary: Fun, easy-to-grow plant famous for its pancake-shaped leaves. Propagates readily from small plantlets.

description: A large tree species native to Southeast Asia with thick, glossy, oval leaves. Popular burgundy and variegated cultivars available.
careSummary: Fast-growing and easy to maintain. Wipe leaves occasionally to keep them shiny and healthy.

soilType: Coarse orchid bark with perlite and sphagnum moss
description: One of the most popular orchids for indoor growing, native to Southeast Asia. Produces long-lasting flowers on graceful arching stems.
careSummary: Long-blooming orchid that thrives in bright indirect light. Water by soaking bark and letting it drain completely.

soilType: Aroid mix with orchid bark and perlite
description: A climbing aroid from Central and South America with elongated, holey leaves. More delicate and compact than Monstera deliciosa.
careSummary: A more compact cousin of the Monstera deliciosa with fenestrated leaves that develop more holes as it grows.

soilType: Airy, well-draining mix with perlite and orchid bark
description: A vining epiphyte from Eastern Asia with thick waxy leaves and clusters of star-shaped, fragrant pink flowers.
careSummary: Easy vine that thrives on bright light and prefers to be root-bound to bloom. Produces stunning porcelain-like flowers.

description: A stunning Brazilian plant with oval leaves featuring intricate patterns resembling peacock feathers in light and dark green.
careSummary: Requires high humidity and distilled water to prevent leaf browning. Its intricate leaf patterns are absolutely worth the effort.

soilType: Very well-draining succulent mix with coarse sand
description: A succulent shrub native to South Africa with thick, glossy oval leaves that develop red edges in bright sun. Can grow into a small tree.
careSummary: A classic succulent that thrives on bright light and sparse watering. Can live for decades and grow into an impressive specimen.

description: A classic trailing houseplant native to Central America with heart-shaped leaves. One of the most adaptable and forgiving houseplants.
careSummary: Extremely low-maintenance trailing plant that tolerates low light and irregular watering. Perfect for shelves and hanging baskets.
